title: philadelphus grandiflorus var. undulatus
intro: Philadelphus grandiflorus var. undulatus, commonly known as the fragrant or sweet-scented mock orange, is a flowering plant in the family Hydrangeaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with potential uses in treating respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions. The fragrant flowers and leaves of Philadelphus grandiflorus var. undulatus have been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce stress, and they may also have antibacterial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the essential oil in aromatherapy applications.
